description: Letter of appeal for charity from Yeḥezqel b. Ibrāhīm (the writer) and the former judge Moshe b. Shemarya to Abū ʿImran Mūsā b. Abī l-Ḥayy, Segulat ha-Yeshiva. Moshe has gone blind from ophthalmia (ramad); his eye is white and he walks with a cane. Yehezqel is so infirm that he has not left his home for two years, even to attend synagogue services. They ask Mūsā to intervene on their behalf with the Nagid Sar ha-Sarim (Mevorakh b. Saadya), though they know that Mevorakh is busy with the “service of the rulers” (khidmat al-salāṭīn), see Rustow, Heresy, p. 339, and Cohen, Jewish Self-Government, p. 220. See also ENA 2805.5a, in which Natan b. Nahray informs Musa that as instructed he has given 1 dinar each to Moshe the Judge and Yeḥezqel the Alexandrian, who is sick and confined to his house. Dated after 1094. (Information in part from Goitein’s note cards) ASE
tags: illness: housebound, old age, illness letter 969-1517, Illness, 11th c, disability, disability: vision, Appeal, illness: eye, cane
description_he: מכתב בקשת צדקה מיחזקאל ב. אברהים ומהשופט לשעבר משה ב. שמריה לאבו עמראן מוסא ב. אבי אל-חי. משה עיוור מדלקת עיניים. יחזקאל לא יצא מביתו שנתיים. מבקשים התערבות אצל הנגיד מבורך ב. סעדיה
